pages.name: Home
pages.description: Landing page introducing the cultural food and music festival and directing users to key tasks, with today’s highlights and quick access to tickets, schedule, food, activities, map, donate, and planner views.
primary_functions: Highlight primary actions such as buying tickets, viewing schedule, exploring food, activities, and map
Provide quick links to current day schedule, featured artists, featured dishes, and donation campaign
Surface shortcuts to My Planner views (My Schedule, Tasting List, My Lineup, Family Plan, My Map/Saved Locations)

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click primary "Buy Tickets" CTA or Tickets card
Click "View Today’s Schedule" from schedule preview
Click "Explore Lineup" from featured artists
Click "Explore Food & Vendors" from featured dishes section
Click "Workshops & Activities" teaser
Click "Open Festival Map" CTA
Click "Support {campaign name}" from donation highlight
Click "Go to My Schedule" shortcut
Click "Go to Tasting List" shortcut
Click "Go to My Lineup" shortcut
Click "Go to Family Plan" shortcut
Click "Go to My Map" shortcut

access_methods.description: Main navigation entry labeled "Home"
Default landing URL from search engines and marketing links

pages.name: Tickets
pages.description: Central place to browse and select festival passes and ticket options, compare tiers, and add selections to the cart within a budget.
primary_functions: Display available ticket types (2-day pass, single-day, child pass, etc.) with dates and descriptions
Allow users to set quantities for each ticket type (e.g., 1 adult 2-day pass and 1 child 2-day pass)
Provide tier/section selection and sorting/filtering by price to find the cheapest option
Show running subtotal and constraints (e.g., combined total under a given budget) before adding to cart
Add selected ticket combinations to the cart

incoming_params.description: Optional pass type to pre-select (e.g., 'two_day', 'single_day').
Optional day to highlight when showing single-day ticket options (e.g., 'saturday').

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click "View Cart" or cart icon after adding tickets

access_methods.description: Header navigation link labeled "Tickets"
Buttons and CTAs from Home, About, and FAQ sections deep-linking into ticket options
Optional URL parameters used to pre-select ticket type or day

pages.name: Cart
pages.description: Review and adjust items selected for purchase before checkout.
primary_functions: List all tickets currently in the cart with quantities, tiers/sections, and prices
Allow users to update quantities or remove ticket types
Show subtotal and estimated total cost
Provide actions to continue shopping (back to Tickets or other sections) or proceed to checkout

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click "Continue Shopping"
Click "Proceed to Checkout"

access_methods.description: Cart icon/button in the site chrome linking directly to cart.html

pages.name: Checkout
pages.description: Collect delivery and purchaser details for the current cart and advance to the payment details step without processing real payments.
primary_functions: Display order summary including selected tickets and totals
Allow users to choose delivery method (Mobile Ticket/E-ticket vs physical options) with Mobile/E-ticket clearly labeled
Collect basic purchaser details (name, email, phone) if needed for ticket delivery
Advance users through an on-page stepper to the payment details step after clicking Proceed/Continue, without requiring actual payment completion

access_methods.description: Reached by clicking "Proceed to Checkout" from cart.html

pages.name: Schedule
pages.description: Timetable of all performances and scheduled events across festival days and stages, supporting filtering by day, time range, and stage, and linking into event details and My Schedule.
primary_functions: Display performances in a grid or list view with day selector (e.g., Friday, Saturday, Sunday)
Provide time range filters (e.g., show events between 2:00 PM and 10:00 PM) and stage filters (e.g., Main Stage)
Allow users to open individual event detail pages from performance cards
Indicate which performances are already saved in the user's My Schedule

incoming_params.description: Initial day to display (e.g., 'sunday') for quick access to a specific festival day.
Optional filter start time in 'HH:MM' format (e.g., '14:00').
Optional filter end time in 'HH:MM' format (e.g., '22:00').
Optional stage to pre-select in the stage filter.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on a performance card in the schedule
Click "Go to My Schedule" from schedule page

access_methods.description: Header navigation link labeled "Schedule"
Deep links from Home or Planner specifying day and optional time range

pages.name: Event Detail
pages.description: Detailed view of a single performance or scheduled festival event, with artist and stage context and controls to manage My Schedule.
primary_functions: Show event information including artist name, stage, start/end time, description, and day
Provide an Add to My Schedule / Remove from My Schedule button to manage personal schedule
Optionally link to the artist detail page or stage/map location

incoming_params.description: ID of the performance whose details should be displayed.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click "View My Schedule" after adding/removing performance
Click on artist name to jump to lineup filtered to same day
Click stage location link to open map focused on this stage

access_methods.description: Accessed by clicking performances from Schedule, Home schedule preview, or My Schedule

pages.name: Lineup
pages.description: Browse all festival artists and their performance slots, with support for headliner/local filters and time windows to build personal lineups.
primary_functions: List artists with filters by day, time (e.g., after 6:00 PM), stage, and artist type (headliner, local/emerging)
Highlight artists’ performance times and stages at a glance
Allow users to sort or prioritize headliners or local artists as needed
Open artist detail pages from artist cards

incoming_params.description: Day to filter artists by (e.g., 'saturday').
Earliest performance start time to include, in 'HH:MM' (e.g., '18:00').
Latest performance start time to include, in 'HH:MM'.
Optional artist type filter value (e.g., 'headliner', 'local').

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on an artist card
Click "Go to My Lineup"

access_methods.description: Header navigation link labeled "Lineup"
Deep links for specific day/time/type views (e.g., Saturday night headliners)

pages.name: Artist Detail
pages.description: Information hub for a specific artist or band, including bio, cultural background, performance times, and favorite/lineup controls.
primary_functions: Display artist bio, genre, photos, and cultural background
Show all scheduled performance times and stages for that artist
Provide an Add to Lineup / Add to Favorites button to save the artist to My Lineup
Indicate if the artist is a headliner or local/emerging act

incoming_params.description: ID of the artist whose details should be displayed.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click "View My Lineup" after adding artist to favorites
Click a performance time row to view that day’s schedule

access_methods.description: Accessed by clicking artist cards from Lineup, Home, or My Lineup views

pages.name: Food & Vendors
pages.description: Directory of food vendors and their dishes, focusing on discovery by cuisine, dietary needs, price, and ratings, with links into detailed dishes and the map.
primary_functions: List dishes and/or vendors with filters for dish type (mains, dessert, drinks), cuisine, price range, dietary labels (e.g., vegetarian), and rating
Allow users to set a maximum price (e.g., $15) and filter items by minimum rating (e.g., 4.0+ stars)
Enable users to view dish detail pages from product cards
Optionally show vendor location or a shortcut to view on the festival map

incoming_params.description: Initial dish type filter (e.g., 'main', 'dessert', 'drink').
Optional cuisine to filter dishes by (e.g., 'Mexican').
Maximum price for dishes (e.g., 15 for tasks under $15).
Dietary filter tag (e.g., 'vegetarian').
Minimum rating filter for dishes (e.g., 4.0).

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on a dish card
Click "View on Map" on a vendor or dish
Click "View Tasting List" shortcut

access_methods.description: Header navigation link labeled "Food"
Deep links into pre-filtered vegetarian, cuisine, or dish-type views

pages.name: Dish Detail
pages.description: Detailed information about a specific dish offered at the festival, including vendor info, price, dietary tags, ratings, and tasting list controls.
primary_functions: Display dish name, cuisine, description, ingredients, dietary tags, vendor name, and price
Show average rating and reviews summary
Provide an Add to Tasting List / Remove from Tasting List button
Optionally show where the vendor is located on the festival map or link to the map

incoming_params.description: ID of the dish whose details should be displayed.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click "View Tasting List" after adding a dish
Click vendor location link to open the map near this stall
Click "Back to Food & Vendors"

access_methods.description: Accessed by clicking dish cards from Food & Vendors or Tasting List

pages.name: Activities & Workshops
pages.description: Listing of all non-performance activities, including dance workshops and family/kids activities, with rich filters for day, time, price, level, rating, and family-friendliness.
primary_functions: List activities and workshops with filters by category/type (dance, kids, family-friendly, cultural, etc.)
Provide day filter (e.g., Saturday, Sunday) and time filters (e.g., before 3:00 PM or between 12:00 PM and 5:00 PM)
Allow users to filter by price (including a Free option) and level (e.g., intermediate) where applicable
Provide rating filter (e.g., 4.5+ stars) and open activity/workshop detail pages from cards

incoming_params.description: Initial day to show for activities (e.g., 'saturday', 'sunday').
Filter for activity type (e.g., 'dance_workshop', 'kids_activity', 'family_activity').
Earliest start time to include (e.g., '12:00').
Latest start time to include (e.g., '17:00').
Maximum price filter (0 for free activities/workshops).
Skill level filter (e.g., 'intermediate').
Minimum rating filter for activities (e.g., 4.5).
If true, pre-filters to kids/family-focused activities.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on an activity or workshop card
Click "View Family Plan" shortcut

access_methods.description: Header navigation link labeled "Activities"
Deep links to pre-filtered views like free Saturday dance workshops or Sunday kids activities

pages.name: Activity / Workshop Detail
pages.description: Detailed view for a single activity or workshop, including dance classes and kids activities, with registration and family plan controls.
primary_functions: Show activity details such as type (e.g., dance workshop, kids activity), description, instructor/host, location, day, time, and rating
For workshops, display and submit a registration form capturing name, email, and phone number
For family/kids activities, provide Add to Family Plan / Add to My Schedule actions
Confirm successful registration or saving to the user’s plan without requiring authentication

incoming_params.description: ID of the activity or workshop whose details should be displayed.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click "View Family Plan" after adding a kids/family activity
Click "Back to Activities"
Click location link to view activity area on the map

access_methods.description: Accessed from Activities listings or Family Plan items via activityId
Workshop registration form posts within the page using registerForWorkshop

pages.name: Festival Map
pages.description: Interactive map of the festival grounds with stages, food vendors, and other key locations, with tools to find nearby stalls and pin them into a personal route.
primary_functions: Display a navigable map with search to locate places such as the Main Stage
Provide map filters to show/hide categories including food vendors by type (mains/meals, dessert, drinks/beverages)
Allow users to select vendor markers near a chosen location and Pin / Add to Route those vendors
Open a My Route / Saved Locations panel associated with the map where users can view pinned spots and reorder them (e.g., mains first, dessert second, drinks third)

incoming_params.description: MapLocation ID to center the map on (e.g., Main Stage or a vendor stall).
Optional focus mode such as 'food_near_main_stage' or 'my_route'.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click "Open My Route" from the saved locations panel
Click "View menu" from a food vendor card in the map panel

access_methods.description: Header navigation link labeled "Map"
Deep links to map centered on specific stages or vendors

pages.name: Donate
pages.description: Donation form for supporting festival programs and specific funds, including Youth Music Education, with support for preset amounts and newsletter opt-in.
primary_functions: Offer preset donation amounts (including a $25 option) and a custom amount field
Provide a fund/campaign dropdown (e.g., Youth Music Education) for directing the donation
Allow users to select Credit Card as the payment method and enter placeholder card details if required
Collect donor name and email, offer a Subscribe to Newsletter opt-in checkbox, and submit the donation form without external confirmation flows

incoming_params.description: Pre-selected donation fund ID (e.g., Youth Music Education).
Optional initial donation amount to pre-fill (e.g., 25).

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click "Back to Festival" after completing the donation form

access_methods.description: Header navigation link labeled "Donate"
Promotional banners from Home or About deep-linking into donation page with a specific fund selected
Donation form submission handled on-page via submitDonation

pages.name: My Planner
pages.description: Single hub for all of the user’s saved plans: schedule, tasting list, lineup, family plan, and saved locations, with tabbed views and tools for curating lists like a Saturday Night lineup.
primary_functions: Provide tabbed or segmented views such as My Schedule, Tasting List, My Lineup, Family Plan, and My Map/Saved Locations
Display all performances the user has added to My Schedule and allow basic reordering or removal
Show saved dishes in the Tasting List and allow users to review and remove items
Display saved artists in My Lineup and allow creation/renaming of custom lists (e.g., Saturday Night) and assignment of artists to those lists
Show family-friendly activities saved to the Family Plan and allow adjustments
Optionally mirror the user’s saved map locations (from the Map page) in a My Map/Saved Locations section for reference

incoming_params.description: Determines which tab is active: 'my_schedule', 'tasting_list', 'my_lineup', 'family_plan', or 'my_map'.
Optional custom artist list to open, e.g., a 'Saturday Night' lineup list.
Optional day to highlight within My Schedule or Family Plan previews.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on a performance in My Schedule view
Click on a dish in Tasting List view
Click on an artist in My Lineup view
Click on an activity in Family Plan view
Click "View on Map" for a saved location in My Map/Saved Locations view
Click "Find more Saturday night artists" from My Lineup view
Click "See full Sunday schedule" from My Schedule or Family Plan views

access_methods.description: Header links labeled "My Schedule", "Tasting List", "My Lineup", "Family Plan", and "My Map" all point to planner.html with different view parameters
Deep links specifying which planner view/tab to show and optionally which custom artist list to open

pages.name: About
pages.description: Background information about the festival and key informational content, including mission, cultural focus, logistics, policies, FAQ, accessibility, and volunteer information.
primary_functions: Explain the festival’s mission, cultural focus, and history
Provide practical information such as location, dates, and general policies
Include subsections for FAQ, accessibility information, terms and conditions, privacy policy, and volunteer information

incoming_params.description: Specific informational section to scroll to: 'faq', 'accessibility', 'privacy_policy', 'terms', or 'volunteer'.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click venue address "View on Map"
Click "Tickets & Admission" link from general info or FAQ
Click "Contact us" from policies or volunteer info
Click "Support our programs" from mission section

access_methods.description: Footer links "About the Festival", "FAQ", "Accessibility", "Privacy Policy", "Terms & Conditions", and "Volunteer" deep-link into this page using the section parameter
Linked from marketing materials or search results as the festival info page
section query parameter controls which subsection is focused on load

pages.name: Contact
pages.description: Contact and support information for festival-goers, including direct contact methods and a general inquiry form.
primary_functions: Provide a contact form for general inquiries
Display email addresses, phone numbers, or on-site help desk details
Offer guidance for common issues (tickets, accessibility, lost and found) and link back to relevant informational sections

incoming_params.description: Pre-selected contact topic (e.g., 'tickets', 'accessibility', 'lost_and_found', 'general').

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a common issue link that points users to FAQ
Click accessibility guidance link
Click "Ticket Help" link for more ticket information

access_methods.description: Footer link labeled "Contact Us"
Contact form submission handled on-page via submitContactForm
topic query parameter pre-fills the subject/topic of the contact form

header_links.description: View performances by day, time, and stage
Explore all artists performing at the festival
Find workshops, kids activities, and other experiences
View interactive festival map and vendors
Support the festival and its programs
Manage your saved artists and custom lineups
View saved locations and routes from the map

footer_links.description: Details on ticket types and policies
Get in touch with the festival team
Information on volunteering at the festival
